corpus_building.implemented_as: The TXT paper is converted into a section-aware annotated mini-corpus.

tokenization.implemented_as: Every concept phrase and sentence is normalized into tokens for counts and n-grams.

normalization.implemented_as: Lower-casing and punctuation cleanup are used for phrase matching and deduplication.

n_gram.implemented_as: Concept length and document top unigrams/bigrams/trigrams are exposed.

bow_and_naive_bayes.implemented_as: Section BoW hits act as class-conditional section evidence for intro/method/experiment/conclusion style decisions.

tf_idf_document_term_matrix.implemented_as: Document-level frequency and TF-IDF matrices expose which section concepts are characteristic of this paper.

laplace_smoothing.implemented_as: P(section | concept) uses add-one smoothing, so unseen section-concept pairs are not forced to zero.

evidence_cue_lexicon.implemented_as: Cue phrases such as propose, achieve, outperform, and BLEU give transparent rule-based evidence hints.

sequence_labeling.implemented_as: The first model contributes BIO boundary evidence for concept phrase extraction.

canonicalization_noisy_channel_style.implemented_as: Observed surface phrases are mapped to canonical terms with an alias confidence score.

location.sentence: 1 I NTRODUCTION Modern text-to-speech (TTS) pipelines are complex (Taylor, 2009).

context_window.previous: In addition, since Tacotron generates speech at the frame level, it’s substantially faster than sample-level au- toregressive methods.
context_window.current: 1 I NTRODUCTION Modern text-to-speech (TTS) pipelines are complex (Taylor, 2009).
context_window.next: For example, it is common for statistical parametric TTS to have a text frontend extracting various linguistic features, a duration model, an acoustic feature prediction model and a complex signal-processing-based vocoder (Zen et al., 2009; Agiomyrgiannakis, 2015).

top_candidates.sentence: We train Tacotron on an internal North American English dataset, which contains about 24.6 hours of speech data spoken by a professional female speaker.
Building these components often requires extensive domain expertise and may contain brittle design choices.
In this paper, we present Tacotron, an end-to-end generative text-to-speech model that synthesizes speech directly from characters.
We present several key techniques to make the sequence-tosequence framework perform well for this challenging task.
We present several key techniques to make the sequence-tosequence framework perform well for this challenging task.
Tacotron achieves a 3.82 subjective 5-scale mean opinion score on US English, outperforming a production parametric system in terms of naturalness.
These components are based on extensive domain expertise and are laborious to design.
There are thus many advantages of an integrated end-to-end TTS system that can be trained on <text, audio> pairs with minimal human annotation.
First, such a system alleviates the need for laborious feature engineering, which may involve heuristics and brittle design choices.
In this paper, we propose Tacotron, an end-to-end generative TTS model based on the sequence-to-sequence (seq2seq) with attention paradigm .
Furthermore, the model is trained on phoneme inputs and the experimental results seem to be somewhat limited.
Char2Wav is an independently-developed end-to-end model that can be trained on characters.
With a very simple waveform synthesis module, it achieves a 3.82 MOS score on US English, outperforming a production parametric system in terms of naturalness.
We have yet to investigate many aspects of our model; many early design decisions have gone unchanged.
